dismissalText.commentary: Ajinkya Rahane c Markram b Mohsin Khan 10 (15b 0x4 1x6 20m) SR: 66.66

dismissalComment.html: sliced up, and Markram sends the KKR skipper on his way! Mohsin doing his thing yet again! Zipped in full and this follows the batter a little outside leg. Rahane wants to go downtown but the bat face opens upon contact. The ball skews off the outer half of the blade and Markram, back-pedalling, snares the chance. KKR in trouble!

dismissalComment.html: plonked to extra cover, and KKR's troubles at the top continue! On a length and agnling across the batter outside off. Seifert looks to go inside-out but does not get under it enough. A rather tame attempt in the end and Mukul gobbles it up. Mohsin with a wicket-maiden to begin, and KKR rocked early, again!

dismissalText.commentary: Angkrish Raghuvanshi obstructing the field 9 (8b 1x4 0x6 15m) SR: 112.5

dismissalComment.html: a huge mix-up, but Raghuvanshi survives! Back of a length on middle and off. Raghuvanshi punches it to mid on and sets off, only to be sent back from half-way. He sprawls across the turf and cops a throw from Shami for his troubles. LSG did indulge in a hint of an appeal but the batter did not change direction to get in line with the throw. Well, that is what it seemed like in real-time but the third umpire has now been called into action. Oh wow. Raghuvanshi's turning radius was more than what might be deemed normal. He did look at the ball too, and the third umpire reckons he changed direction. Raghuvanshi has to go, and he is not at all happy. The key question here was how big his turning radius was. It was a little more than what would be deemed normal, and he ended up outside the cut strip while diving. The third umpire seems to agree with that assessment. KKR three down now!

dismissalText.commentary: Cameron Green c †Pant b Mohsin Khan 34 (21b 0x4 3x6 38m) SR: 161.9

dismissalComment.html: another shank, and this time, Green bites the dust! Seems like another cutter, bashed into the track outside off and angling further away. Green tries to muscle it into the leg side and loses all shape. Plinks it up off the splice and Pant calls early to complete the catch in the point region. KKR five down!

dismissalText.commentary: Anukul Roy c Badoni b Mohsin Khan 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 1m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: a five-for, and Mohsin's dream IPL soars to an even higher cloud! Slanted in full on middle and leg, and this seems to stop in the surface. Bowled at 143.8kph and Anukul, looking to flick it, pops it straight to mid wicket. Simple grab for Badoni in the ring, and Mohsin has five!

dismissalComment.html: a huge mix-up, but Raghuvanshi survives! Back of a length on middle and off. Raghuvanshi punches it to mid on and sets off, only to be sent back from half-way. He sprawls across the turf and cops a throw from Shami for his troubles. LSG did indulge in a hint of an appeal but the batter did not change direction to get in line with the throw. Well, that is what it seemed like in real-time but the third umpire has now been called into action. Oh wow. Raghuvanshi's turning radius was more than what might be deemed normal. He did look at the ball too, and the third umpire reckons he changed direction. Raghuvanshi has to go, and he is not at all happy. The key question here was how big his turning radius was. dismissalComment.html: off stump pegged back, and it is deja-vu for Pooran, all over again! Narine outwits his fellow Trinidadian, and KKR draw first blood! Barely an emotion from Narine. He has done this before. And he is doing it again. Fizzing off-break on a length on leg. Pooran has an ungainly swipe across the line and misses. Hears the rattling of his stumps soon after, and LSG have lost a wicket off the very first ball!

image.longCaption: Aiden Markram player page headshot cutout, 2021

headshotImage.caption: Aiden Markram player page headshot cutout, 2021
headshotImage.longCaption: Aiden Markram player page headshot cutout, 2021

dismissalComment.html: shanked into the air, and the catch will go under Rinku Singh's name yet again! Powell with the acrobatics, and Narine has come up trump in a clutch situation. What a champion. What a bowler! Back of a length on the stumps. Markram looks to muscle it downtown but does not have the swinging room. Clinks it flat towards long on, where Powell runs to his left and takes the catch, only to realize he will step out of bounds. He reverse-flicks it towards Rinku, who has come around from long off, and KKR have LSG on the mat, all over again!
